name: Tortiere#2
recipeInstructions:
1. Finely mince onion and garlic.
2. Peel and grate the potatoes.
3. Place pork, onion, garlic, mace, sage, grated potato, raisins, and pepper in a large heavy pot.
4. Cover with boiling water (about 2 cups) and bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat to medium and cook, uncovered, until meat is no longer pink and water is absorbed, about 30 to 45 minutes. Stir occasionally and reduce water if necessary to avoid boiling.
6. Remove from heat and let cool.
7. Skim off excess fat.
8. Preheat oven to 400°F.
9. Meanwhile, prepare pastry for a pie plate. Line the bottom with half of the pastry and prick with fork. Bake at 400°F for 10 minutes until cooled to room temperature.
10. Pour the cooled meat mixture into the pie shell.
11. Cover with top crust and crimp edges.
12. Cut vents in the center to allow steam to escape.
13. Bake at 400°F for another 10 minutes, then reduce heat to 350°F and bake for an additional 30 minutes or until the crust is light brown and filling is bubbly.
14. Serve hot.
